market_context: US stock futures are holding steady to slightly higher ahead of the Federal Reserve's interest rate decision and PPI data release, with S&P 500 E-mini futures (ESH26) up +0.50% and Nasdaq futures (NQH26) up +0.52% in recent trading.[1][3] Markets are influenced by retreating oil prices amid Iran conflict concerns, strength in chip stocks like WDC (+9.64%) and travel names like ABNB (+2.83%), following Tuesday's gains where S&P 500 closed +0.25%, Dow +0.10%, and Nasdaq +0.51%.[3][4] Pre-market movers highlight tech and AI optimism, with overall sentiment cautious but supported by falling bond yields.[3][8]

predictions.reasoning: S&P 500 E-mini futures (ESH26) are up +0.50% pre-market at 6735.25, signaling positive momentum into open amid retreating oil prices and ahead of Fed decision.[3] Tuesday's S&P 500 close was +0.25%, with chip and travel stocks rallying, supporting broader index strength.[3] Underlying bid noted in live futures commentary, with 200-day moving average providing support.[5]

Nasdaq 100 E-mini futures (NQH26) rose +0.52% recently, with Tuesday's index up +0.51% driven by tech rally including chip makers like WDC (+9.64%) and STX (+5.59%).[3] Wall Street futures rising on tech gains, Meta AI prep boosting sentiment.[8] Pre-market strength in GOOGL (+1.75%), AAPL (+0.56%), and NVDA (+0.25%) points to continued Nasdaq outperformance.[3][4]

Western Digital (WDC) is a top pre-market mover at +9.64% to 313.81, leading chip sector rally alongside STX (+5.59%).[3] Recent sessions show consistent strength in semiconductors like MU (+0.25%), NVDA (+0.25%), with Nasdaq futures supportive.[3][4] Momentum likely persists amid tech optimism pre-Fed.[1]

Airbnb (ABNB) up +2.83% pre-market to 131.95, part of travel stock surge with ALK (+1.33%), UAL (+3.22%).[3] Sector rotation into travel amid falling oil prices supports gains, following Tuesday's broader market advance.[3] Positive futures tone adds tailwind.[1]
